user_query:
I need a research-backed oven calibration test plan and a validation pass on last week’s temperature logs. Please use the inputs in input/, research manufacturer/OEM guidance plus at least one safety/standards source, and produce the outputs below.

Inputs you must use:
- input/device_inventory.json (appliances with IDs, types, manufacturers, and models)
- input/oven_temp_logs.csv (recent setpoint vs. measured data)

Required tasks and outputs:
1) Directory audit: Inspect the input/ directory and write audit/input_manifest.json capturing an array of objects with fields: path (relative path under input/), size_bytes (integer), and sha256 (hex digest). This ensures you actually inspected local files and will be used for cross-checking.

2) Internet research: Perform search engine queries to find oven calibration tolerances and recommended verification steps. Include at least:
   - One source from an official manufacturer website for a brand present in input/device_inventory.json (e.g., Vulcan, Blodgett, or Wolf).
   - One source from a government site or recognized safety/standards organization (e.g., a domain ending in .gov or a well-known standards org).
   Do not paste full URLs in your report; store domains and titles. Save your findings to research/findings.json with this structure:
   {
     "queries": ["string", ...],
     "sources": [
       {"domain": "string", "title": "string", "org_type": "manufacturer|government|standards_org", "key_points": ["string", ...]}
     ],
     "chosen_tolerance_policy": {
       "default_tolerance_f": number,
       "rules": [
         {"type_contains": "string", "tolerance_f": number, "source_domain": "string"}
       ]
     },
     "retrieved_at": "ISO-8601 timestamp"
   }
   Notes:
   - "queries" must list the exact search engine queries you used.
   - "sources" must include at least one manufacturer source that matches a manufacturer in the inventory and at least one government or standards organization source.
   - In "chosen_tolerance_policy", specify a default tolerance in °F and optionally more specific rules by appliance type. You will use these values for validation in step 3.

3) Validation and test writing: Using the logs and your tolerance policy, produce both a machine-readable result and a human-readable test plan:
   - tests/oven_test_results.csv with headers: unit_id,setpoint_f,measured_f,delta_f,tolerance_f,status. Compute delta_f = measured_f - setpoint_f. For each row, choose tolerance_f by applying the most specific rule from chosen_tolerance_policy, else default. Set status = "within_tolerance" if |delta_f| <= tolerance_f, otherwise "calibrate".
   - tests/oven_calibration_testplan.md including: a brief overview; a “Sources consulted” section listing each source’s title and domain; a “Tolerance policy” section citing the chosen values; and a “Step-by-step check” section that describes how to verify and document oven temperature at common setpoints (no brand-specific URLs). Keep it concise and actionable.

4) Draft communication: Create communications/email_to_manager.txt as a ready-to-send plain text email (first line must be a Subject: line). In 120–200 words, summarize:
   - How you formed the plan (mention manufacturer and safety/standards sources without URLs),
   - The number of units that require calibration based on tests/oven_test_results.csv (this count must match the number of rows with status == calibrate),
   - The attachments you will send (the test plan and the CSV results),
   - A simple scheduling request with two proposed time windows.

Constraints and checks:
- Do not hardcode tolerances; derive and record them in research/findings.json based on your sources. The correctness will be checked by the internal consistency between your policy and the computed statuses.
- The Internet research must be performed with search engine queries and reflected in the saved queries and source metadata.
- All output paths must exist exactly as specified and be reproducible locally from the given inputs and your recorded policy.

Deliverables to produce:
- audit/input_manifest.json
- research/findings.json
- tests/oven_test_results.csv
- tests/oven_calibration_testplan.md
- communications/email_to_manager.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
